commands.review_summary:
Adds `docs/predictive-refresh-policy.md`, a decision record for an optional deterministic 2-to-30-minute adaptive refresh frequency while preserving fixed/manual defaults and menu-open retry behavior.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a docs/design PR, not a bug report. Source inspection confirms current main uses the fixed RefreshFrequency timer and missing/error-only menu-open retry baseline that the decision record proposes to evolve.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The next action is maintainer product approval or rejection; there is no narrow automated code/docs repair to queue.

target.title: docs: propose predictive refresh policy
target.body:
## Summary
- Reconcile the proposal with current `UsageStore` batch scheduling and menu-open retry safety.
- Narrow the design to one product decision: an opt-in deterministic 2–30 minute adaptive batch cadence.
- Keep the five-minute default, fixed/manual modes, provider/account selection, and menu-open behavior unchanged.
- Exclude persistent interaction history, per-account prediction, learned ranking, menu prewarming, and a second scheduler.

## Maintainer review

Mechanically ready on head `6d4f3f051104f7b126a845117adfbad96850030d`; held for product sign-off under `VISION.md`.

Decision requested: approve or decline the bounded opt-in experiment. Recommendation: approve the decision record, then implement it as separately reviewed steps. Approval does not approve making adaptive refresh the default or merging PR #1733.

## Validation
- `make check` — pass
- `make test` — pass (44/44 shards)
- `node Scripts/check-documentation-links.mjs` — pass (137 local links)
- `git diff --check` — pass
- Branch autoreview — clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- Exact-head CI — pass (docs-only macOS shard correctly skipped; lint/build tests and both Linux builds passed)

## Notes
Docs-only; no runtime behavior, settings, storage, auth, or release changes.
